cataloging_subject.label: The Middle English Charters of Christ
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The intellectual and content unit selected from Mary Caroline Spalding's 1914 edition and encoded as U-M item AFW1075.0001.001: the Short Charter of Christ, the Long Charter of Christ, and four distinct Appendix II works. Parallel manuscript texts are alternative witnesses or versions of those works, not additional abstract works. The unit is neither a single medieval composition nor Spalding's complete dissertation.

catalog_summary.title: The Middle English Charters of Christ
catalog_summary.author: Anonymous medieval authors
catalog_summary.date: Fourteenth- and fifteenth-century works and witnesses; edited selection published 1914; electronic encoding published 2006
catalog_summary.region: England; the Short Charter is associated with northern English language, while the Long Charter is associated with Midland or southern English language

titles.value: The Middle English Charters of Christ
notes: This is Spalding's publication title and the title of the encoded edition-level selection, not a collective medieval title supplied by the witnesses.
The Latin title labels a Middle English verse work also described as the Kent Charter.
Spalding supplies this Latin title for an incomplete Middle English prose tract related to the Charter of Christ and to The Poor Caitiff.

scope.description: The six medieval intellectual works represented in the encoded selection

notes: This collective display does not assert common authorship; the six works may have different anonymous creators and transmitters.

scope.description: Selection, classification, collation, and arrangement of the Charter texts and Appendix II works

notes: Spalding reprinted Fehr's previously printed Fairfax text.
Spalding reprinted the Kent Charter text supplied through Macray's earlier publication.

assertion.notes: Recorded as Spalding's argued date rather than silently accepted as consensus.
This conflicts with Spalding's late-fourteenth-century proposal; both are retained.

date_statements.display: Fifteenth century in British Library Add MS 21253
Fourteenth century in St John's College MS E.24
No earlier than the 1395 conveyance on whose dorse the poem appears

assertion.notes: The date of the recto legal document is a terminus, not proof that the dorse poem was entered in 1395.

scope.description: Primary language of two Appendix II works; Latin also occurs in headings, formulae, and embedded passages elsewhere

form_statements.description: Four verse works and two prose works, with modern prose editorial matter.

summaries.value: An edition-level selection of medieval devotional works that imagine Christ's Passion and redemption through the form and language of legal charters. It presents the Short and Long Charters in multiple manuscript witnesses or textual versions, followed by four related Latin and Middle English works.

Six intellectual works are represented: the Short Charter of Christ; the Long Charter of Christ; Carta Domini Nostri Iesu Christi; Carta Libera; Carta Dei; and the incomplete prose Carta Celestis Hereditatis. The Short Charter has twelve continuous witness texts plus an attestation block and Harley 6848 variants. The Long Charter has seven encoded A-text witness streams and five encoded B-text streams; the C text is discussed but not continuously encoded.

The U-M encoding retains the edition's title leaves, printed texts on pp. 3-81, and Appendix II on pp. 95-102. It omits the 124-page prefatory study, Appendix I on pp. 83-94, the glossary on pp. 103-104, and residual back matter, representing some omitted spans with empty page placeholders.

Parallel print columns were serialized by OCR/TEI as consecutive DIV2 streams. Those streams are modeled as witnesses or versions beneath two abstract Charter works and assigned zero additional intellectual-item count.

extent.description: Six abstract medieval works occur within seven top-level source regions: title leaves; omitted prefatory matter; Short Charter texts; Long Charter texts; omitted Appendix I; Appendix II; and omitted back matter. Alternative witnesses and A/B textual versions are not counted as additional works. The XML contains 121 LG elements and 3,870 L elements, but those are representation metrics across alternative witnesses and include variant-only lines, so they are not a single-work verse extent.

content_parts.label: Title leaves
content_parts.summary: The retained title and publication leaves of Spalding's edition.

content_parts.label: Omitted front matter and study
content_parts.summary: Spalding's preface, bibliography, and extended introductory study, represented by a 124-page placeholder run rather than encoded text.

content_parts.label: The Short Charter of Christ
content_parts.summary: One anonymous Middle English verse work represented by twelve continuous witness texts, a separate Add Charter 5960 attestation block, and Harley 6848 variant readings.
notes: DIMEV v1.0 record 6769 / NIMEV 4184 supplies the main modern index identity. The METS introduction reports twenty-four surviving manuscripts; Spalding knew thirteen source witnesses for this edition.

content_parts.label: Sloane MS 3292 witness
content_parts.summary: Continuous Short Charter witness text supplied to Spalding through Carleton Brown's transcript.

content_parts.label: Stowe MS 620 witness
content_parts.summary: Continuous Short Charter witness text supplied through Carleton Brown's transcript.

content_parts.label: Add Charter 5960 witness
content_parts.summary: Continuous Short Charter base text printed with Harley 6848 variants.

content_parts.label: Add Charter 5960 attestation and seal block
content_parts.summary: A separate zero-line prose block describing the attestation, strap, seal, and signatures, with Harley 6848 variants.

content_parts.label: Add MS 37049 witness
content_parts.summary: Continuous Short Charter witness text.

content_parts.label: Harley MS 116 witness
content_parts.summary: Continuous Short Charter witness text supplied through Carleton Brown's transcript.

content_parts.label: Add MS 24343 witness
content_parts.summary: Continuous Short Charter witness text.

content_parts.label: Gonville and Caius MS 230 witness
content_parts.summary: Continuous Short Charter witness text; MS 230 is the shelfmark and 215 is James's catalog-entry number.

content_parts.label: Bodleian MS Ashmole 61 witness
content_parts.summary: Continuous Short Charter witness text; the complete manuscript facsimile is public and cached, and this copy omits the final four lines found in fuller witnesses.

content_parts.label: Harley MS 237 witness
content_parts.summary: Continuous Short Charter witness text.

content_parts.label: Fairfax / Add MS 5465 witness
content_parts.summary: Continuous Short Charter witness text reprinted from B. Fehr's earlier edition.

locators.value: Spalding ff. 119r-122r; DIMEV begins at f. 118

content_parts.label: Bodleian MS Ashmole 189 witness
content_parts.summary: Continuous Short Charter witness text with an unresolved historical/current foliation crosswalk.

locators.value: XML milestones ff. 109r-110r; current catalog Short Charter item at f. 109

content_parts.label: St John's College MS B.15 witness
content_parts.summary: Continuous Short Charter witness text supplied through Alfred Rogers's transcript.

content_parts.label: Harley MS 6848 variants
content_parts.summary: Variant readings and attestation details collated against Add Charter 5960 rather than a thirteenth continuous encoded witness text.

content_parts.label: The Long Charter of Christ
content_parts.summary: One anonymous Middle English verse work represented by seven encoded A-text witness streams and five encoded B-text witness streams; a C text is classified and discussed but not continuously encoded.

content_parts.label: Long Charter A text
content_parts.summary: Seven encoded witness streams; Spalding's parallel print columns are serialized into consecutive XML divisions.
notes: DIMEV v1.0 record 2859 / NIMEV 1718 supplies the modern A-text index identity. The Vernon manuscript is a collateral A-text witness with a complete cached facsimile, but it is not one of the seven continuous encoded streams.

content_parts.label: Rawlinson poet. 175 A-text witness
content_parts.summary: Complete encoded A-text witness stream.

content_parts.label: Add MS 11307 A-text witness
content_parts.summary: Complete encoded A-text witness stream.

content_parts.label: Bodleian MS Bodl. 89 A-text witness
content_parts.summary: Complete encoded A-text witness stream.

content_parts.label: Harley MS 2346 A-text witness
content_parts.summary: Complete encoded A-text witness stream; the XML preserves an anomalous f. 53 then f. 52 milestone sequence.

content_parts.label: Bodleian MS Add. C. 280 A-text witness
content_parts.summary: Complete encoded A-text witness stream with a broader current catalog item boundary.

locators.value: Spalding/XML ff. 124r-125v; current catalog item ff. 124-127

content_parts.label: Harley MS 5396 A-text witness
content_parts.summary: Complete encoded A-text witness stream.

content_parts.label: Magdalen St Peter-in-the-East 18e A-text fragment
content_parts.summary: A 63-line fragment on the verso, printed from H. A. Wilson's supplied transcript and description.

content_parts.label: Long Charter B text
content_parts.summary: Five encoded witness streams; Spalding's parallel print columns are serialized into consecutive XML divisions.
notes: DIMEV v1.0 record 6650 / NIMEV 4154 supplies the modern B-text index identity.

content_parts.label: CUL MS Ii.3.26 B-text witness
content_parts.summary: Complete encoded B-text witness stream with an unresolved historical/current foliation crosswalk.

content_parts.label: CUL MS Ii.4.9 B-text witness
content_parts.summary: Incomplete encoded B-text witness beginning at source line 69.

content_parts.label: Cotton Caligula A II B-text witness
content_parts.summary: Complete encoded B-text witness stream supplied through Carleton Brown's transcript.

content_parts.label: CUL MS Ff.2.38 B-text witness
content_parts.summary: Complete encoded B-text witness stream with an unresolved historical/current foliation crosswalk.

content_parts.label: CUL MS Ee.2.15 B-text witness
content_parts.summary: Encoded B-text witness beginning at source line 69, with an unresolved historical/current foliation crosswalk.

content_parts.label: Long Charter C text context
content_parts.summary: The unique C-text witness in Royal MS 17 C XVII is classified and discussed in Spalding's omitted study and Appendix I but is not encoded as a continuous text.

content_parts.label: Omitted Appendix I
content_parts.summary: Spalding's first appendix, represented by page placeholders rather than encoded text.

content_parts.label: Appendix II: Additional Texts
content_parts.summary: Four additional, distinct charter-related works in Latin and Middle English.

content_parts.label: Carta Domini Nostri Iesu Christi
content_parts.summary: A Latin prose charter and application from British Library Add MS 21253.

content_parts.label: Carta Libera
content_parts.summary: A Latin verse charter based on St John's E.24, with D.8 variants and two D.8-only lines.

locators.value: 1 LG; 38 L, comprising 36 base lines and 2 D.8-only lines

content_parts.label: Carta Dei
content_parts.summary: A Middle English verse charter on the dorse of Bodleian Kent Charter 233, reprinted by Spalding through W. D. Macray's earlier text.

locators.value: 1 LG; 43 L, including a terminal Latin colophon; DIMEV describes 42 verse lines

content_parts.label: Carta Celestis Hereditatis
content_parts.summary: An incomplete Middle English prose tract or excerpt from CUL Ff.6.34, related by Spalding to The Poor Caitiff and ending in an editorial ellipsis.

locators.value: editorial argument plus 2 source-text P; incomplete ending

content_parts.label: Omitted glossary and back matter
content_parts.summary: The glossary and residual back matter, represented by empty page placeholders rather than encoded text.

related_works.title: The Poor Caitiff
notes: Spalding relates Carta Celestis Hereditatis to material in The Poor Caitiff; the encoded excerpt remains cataloged as its own bounded work here.

related_works.title: Long Charter of Christ, C text
notes: The unique Royal MS 17 C XVII C text is related to the cataloged Long Charter work but is not continuously encoded in afw1075.

related_works.title: Charter allegory in Fasciculus Morum
notes: An analogue in the broader medieval Charter-of-Christ tradition, not a source witness of the encoded texts.

related_works.title: William Herebert's Charter-of-Christ lyric
notes: A related early-fourteenth-century charter lyric, not identical with the Short Charter represented here.

open_questions.question: Should the Short Charter's composition be dated to the late fourteenth century, as Spalding argued, or probably to the fifteenth century, as the modern METS introduction proposes?
next_steps: Review the full modern bibliography and manuscript stratigraphy.
Keep composition date separate from surviving-witness dates.
Retain both sourced proposals until the conflict can be adjudicated.

open_questions.question: How do Spalding's historical folio references crosswalk to current catalog foliation for Ashmole 189, Add. C. 280, CUL Ii.3.26, Ff.2.38, and Ee.2.15?
next_steps: Inspect complete current facsimiles or repository foliation maps.
Preserve both historical and current locators in the meantime.
Determine whether each discrepancy reflects rebinding, renumbering, missing leaves, or catalog scope.

notes: This manifest catalogs a six-work edited selection, not one medieval work and not the complete 1914 dissertation.
Alternative manuscript witness streams and Long Charter A/B versions have zero additional intellectual-item count.
The XML's 3,870 L elements are a representation metric across alternative witnesses and variant-only lines, not the line count of a single poem.
Harley 6848 contributes variants and attestation details rather than a thirteenth continuous Short Charter witness stream.
The Long Charter C text is supporting context only because it is not continuously encoded in afw1075.
Appendix II contains four distinct works; Carta Celestis Hereditatis is incomplete and ends with an editorial ellipsis.
